pattern: Weaving a clone directly after each original node turns every 'find the copy of this random target' lookup into a one-hop neighbor read instead of a separate map from old node to new node.
hints: A hashmap from each original node to its freshly built clone solves this in one linear pass; the tighter version asks for constant extra space beyond the output itself.
Splice a duplicate of every node in directly after its original, so for any node the expression original.random.next always names the clone of original.random.
Wire up every clone's random pointer using that interleaved neighbor, then unweave the two lists apart in a second pass, leaving the original list exactly as it was.

edge_inputs.description: mixed random pointers, some forward, some backward, one null
every node's random points to its own predecessor
single node whose random points to itself
every random pointer is null — the clone must not invent links
every random pointer fans in to the same last node
identical values on every node — random resolution must key off node identity, not value

stress.description: random-length list with random pointers scattered across the chain roughly 70% of the time
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(0, max(1, min(size, 500)))
    pairs = []
    for _ in range(n):
        val = rng.randint(-10000, 10000)
        r = rng.randint(0, n - 1) if n and rng.random() < 0.7 else None
        pairs.append([val, r])
    return [pairs]
